text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town nestled between rolling hills and dense forests, there lived a plump, middle-aged librarian named Clara. Clara spent her days organizing books into ever-finer categories: "Cosmode" for those outfits worn on holidays; "Scentagram" for candles that emitted a particular fragrance; "Tunejoy" for compositions that brightened one's spirits. You see, Clara had the most extraordinary ability to categorize everything wonderfully.

Every Sunday afternoon, Clara would organize her vast collection into a beautifully displayed toprichtoire—a circular showcase made of chestnut wood. Her passion for organization was passed down from her late grandmother, who owned the very first town library decades ago.

One afternoon, as usual, Clara got wrapped up in her task and spent far longer than she intended sorting through a particularly sticky-smelling chapter of cookbook poems. She glanced at the clock and sighed. It was time for her weekly social outing at the café, her sanctuary where she could pour
